title: Resolve FUSE-vs-git docs/* asymmetry — workspace-sync mirrors clone → FUSE for the four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files; integrity check stops false-positiving on stale FUSE mtime
target: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (Step 3 — extend universal-pusher follow-up to mirror NEVER_PUSH docs/* clone → FUSE)
monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md (Section 9 'Build Reproducibility' — add cross-check + downgrade rule for build_drift when git HEAD matches data)
test.js (lines 285-289 — replace mtime-comparison with deterministic content equivalence check)

problem.summary: Integrity flagged docs/index.html as critically stale on 2026-05-13T01:16Z based on FUSE mtimes, but git HEAD's docs/index.html had been correctly rebuilt 7+ hours earlier (commit ccf87ec9) and the live GitHub Pages surface served the corrected content. Root cause: workspace-sync rebuilds docs/index.html in its OWN clone (Step 3 universal-pusher follow-up), commits, pushes — but does NOT copy the rebuild back to FUSE. The only writer of FUSE's docs/index.html is operator-driven `node build.js publish`. So FUSE mtime reflects 'time of last operator publish', not 'time of last actual rebuild', and any FUSE-side check (test.js mtime assertion at line 288, integrity Section 9 build-diff) sees an artificially-stale view.
evidence: Integrity report 2026-05-13T01-16: 'docs/index.html does not match a fresh build from current source data. sections.json was updated at 2026-05-12T17:22Z but HTML was last published at 2026-05-12T13:22Z.' Status: critical, build_drift, suggested_fix='node build.js publish'.

git log of origin/main shows commit ccf87ec9 (workspace-sync's universal-pusher rebuild) landed the corrected docs/index.html on 2026-05-12T18:05:34Z — 7 hours BEFORE integrity flagged the file as stale.

test.js lines 286-289: const htmlStat = fs.statSync(HTML_PATH); const winsStat = fs.statSync(WINS_PATH); assert(htmlStat.mtimeMs >= winsStat.mtimeMs - 1000, 'HTML is not stale (modified after or with wins.json)'). Both stats are FUSE-side. When workspace-sync writes data/wins.json to FUSE (universal-pusher rescue) but does NOT write docs/index.html, this check fires false-positive.

monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 3 (lines 432-446): 'if [ -n "$DATA_STAGED" ]; then ... node build.js html ... git add docs/index.html ...' — the rebuild output is staged for commit but never copied to ${WORKSPACE}.

build.js OWNERSHIP table (lines 64-67) classifies all four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files as 'git'-owned, which means `build.js publish` copies git → workspace at publish time. Outside of operator-driven publish, the workspace copies drift.

Secondary observation per the directive: integrity's report wording 'Live site has WRONG DOI' implies a fetch of the published GitHub Pages surface, but the report is actually comparing FUSE HTML vs FUSE data. The 'wrong DOI' claim was inferred from FUSE state, not from what the live site serves. (Both the staleness check and the content claim are operating on the wrong source.)
problem.blast_radius: Recurring false-positive critical on every integrity run that follows a workspace-sync universal-pusher cycle without an operator-driven publish in between. With workspace-sync running hourly and operator publishes typically once per day, this is the dominant pattern. Operator wakes up to 'critical' alerts that aren't critical, eroding trust in integrity's signal. Other FUSE-reading consumers (operator-Claude debugging, test.js when run from FUSE) see the same stale view.
problem.frequency_estimate: Likely false-positive on most overnight integrity runs in 2026-05 since the universal-pusher rebuild path landed (HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-002 era). Specifically observable in the 2026-05-13T01:16Z report; pattern likely present across multiple recent reports though tinker did not pull a multi-week sample for this PROP.

fuse_vs_git_asymmetry_table.purpose: Directive task_3 deliverable. Identifies which docs/* artifacts share the same FUSE-vs-git drift risk so the fix covers all of them, not just the canonical case.

rows.drift_risk: HIGH — multiple git writers, only operator-publish writes FUSE. Most-frequent drift case; the 2026-05-13T01:16Z incident.

rows.drift_risk: MEDIUM — Section 6 explicitly checks for 'stale counts in llms.txt'. If workspace-sync universal-pusher updates FUSE wins.json (new count) but llms.txt was last operator-published with old count, integrity sees a count mismatch. Lower frequency than docs/index.html because llms.txt isn't rebuilt on every data change, but same architectural drift.
rows.covered_by_this_prop: Yes — sibling coverage in mirror loop.

rows.drift_risk: LOW — content rarely changes (URLs are stable). Drift only matters when a new URL is added on the social side; until operator publishes, FUSE shows the old URL list.
rows.covered_by_this_prop: Yes — sibling coverage in mirror loop.

rows.drift_risk: VERY LOW — file is essentially static. Drift only matters when sitemap URL changes. Realistically zero observed false-positives, but architecturally identical to the others.

fuse_vs_git_asymmetry_table.summary: Four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files share the asymmetric drift pattern. docs/index.html is the canonical / high-frequency case. The other three drift less often but break the same way. The fix is a single mirror loop covering all four, not a per-file patch.

option_A_integrity_reads_git.summary: Integrity does its own git fetch (raw URL or fresh clone) and compares git HEAD docs/index.html to staged data files. test.js mtime check stays as-is (or is rewritten to use git HEAD).
pros: Cleanest single-source-of-truth semantic: git is canonical for docs/, integrity reads git.
No new FUSE writes — stays inside the existing 'git owns docs/, FUSE is downstream' model.
Aligns with decider/curmudgeon pattern (clone fresh, never trust FUSE for canonicality).
Integrity now genuinely checks the published surface, not a side-cache.
cons: Adds clone-or-curl overhead per integrity run (acceptable but not zero).

Asymmetric for integrity itself: it reads docs/* from git but data/* from FUSE for other checks. Cross-source comparisons are possible but require care to avoid the inverse mismatch (FUSE data fresh, git docs/ slightly behind by ≤1 hour pull lag).

Does NOT fix the underlying FUSE inconsistency. Other FUSE consumers (test.js when run from FUSE; operator-Claude debugging from the workspace) still see stale docs/index.html relative to fresh data. The asymmetry persists, just hidden from the integrity check.
test.js's HTML-mtime assertion either becomes a misleading FUSE-only check or needs to be rewritten (in which case much of B's work is also needed).
option_A_integrity_reads_git.operator_lean: Operator-stated lean (per directive 'Probably option A — cleanest semantics'). Acknowledged as the closest-to-pure-architecture choice.

option_B_workspace_sync_mirrors_to_FUSE.summary: workspace-sync's existing Step 3 universal-pusher follow-up is extended with a small mirror loop: after the docs/index.html rebuild and any decider-committed sibling files (llms.txt, sitemap.xml, robots.txt), copy each NEVER_PUSH docs/* file from clone → FUSE if FUSE differs. Integrity's existing check works correctly because FUSE catches up within ≤1 hour of any git change.
pros: Fixes the asymmetry at its source. FUSE becomes consistent — same source data, same rendered output, both visible from FUSE.
ONE-LINE primary edit for docs/index.html (the operator-stated bar for preferring B), plus a small mirror loop (~10 lines) that covers the four NEVER_PUSH siblings with no per-file branches.

Architecturally symmetric with build.js publish: that flow already does git → FUSE for all git-owned files. Workspace-sync extending the same direction for NEVER_PUSH docs/* is a continuation, not a new direction.
Other FUSE consumers also benefit — operator-Claude inspecting the workspace sees a fresh docs/index.html. test.js's mtime check stays valid because FUSE is genuinely fresh.
No FUSE→git direction added: smart_copy's NEVER_PUSH guard already prevents reverse flow on these files.
Self-bounding: workspace-sync runs hourly, so max FUSE staleness is ~1 hour even for sibling files committed by the decider between cycles.
cons: Adds writes to FUSE for git-owned files. Mitigation: the writes are clone → FUSE (same direction as build.js publish for git-owned files); the deny-list on smart_copy still prevents the reverse direction. CLAUDE.md File Ownership rules need a one-paragraph addition explaining workspace-sync as a second clone→FUSE writer for these specific files.
Slightly larger diff than option A's 'change the integrity prompt' minimal version — but only if A's test.js change is skipped. With test.js fixed, A is similar in size.

If workspace-sync has a bug that writes wrong content to FUSE for these files, that bug hits FUSE consumers immediately. Mitigation: workspace-sync writes the SAME bytes it just pushed to git; if git is wrong, FUSE is wrong, but git is the published surface so the bug would manifest there too. No new failure mode.
option_B_workspace_sync_mirrors_to_FUSE.operator_lean: Operator-stated 'close second if the workspace-sync edit is small.' This PROP confirms the edit is small (one-line + small loop).

option_C_integrity_fetches_live_site.summary: Integrity web_fetches https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ on each run, parses for citation/count strings, asserts they match expected values from data files.
pros: Tests the actual user-facing surface (most thorough).
Catches GitHub Pages deployment failures (rare but real).
cons: External-network dependency on a per-run health check that should be self-contained.
GitHub Pages deployment lag (~minutes after git push) creates a new false-positive class: 'git is right but live site hasn't deployed yet'.
Requires HTML parsing of a 1.5MB page on every check; slower than file diffs.
Possible rate limits or transient 5xx from GH Pages.
Doesn't fix the asymmetry; just adds a third source of truth.
option_C_integrity_fetches_live_site.operator_lean: Operator-stated 'most thorough but introduces external-network dependency on a per-run health check that should be self-contained.' Implicit decline.

option_hybrid_B_with_A_defense_in_depth.summary: Option B as the primary fix, plus a small option-A flavor in integrity: when build_drift would fire, integrity additionally curl-fetches git HEAD docs/index.html via raw.githubusercontent.com and compares; if git HEAD matches the freshly-rebuilt content, the finding is downgraded from 'critical' to 'informational: FUSE is stale relative to git, will reconcile within ~1h on next workspace-sync'.
pros: Even if workspace-sync hasn't caught up yet (e.g., decider just committed docs/llms.txt and integrity runs 5 minutes later), integrity correctly distinguishes 'live site is wrong' from 'FUSE just hasn't synced yet'.
The downgrade is conservative — still surfaces the FUSE staleness as informational so it's debuggable.
No extra network load when workspace-sync is keeping FUSE current (the curl runs only when the FUSE-side check would have flagged build_drift).
cons: Adds a few lines to structure-integrity.md.
Two-step check is harder to reason about than option B alone.

recommendation.rationale: Operator's preference framing was 'simplest semantic alignment, not smallest diff.' Pure option A is simpler-of-diff but leaves the FUSE inconsistency in place — every FUSE consumer (not just integrity) sees stale rendered HTML against fresh data. Option B fixes the inconsistency at its source with a one-line primary edit and a tight loop for siblings. The defense-in-depth A flavor adds ~5 lines to integrity that protect against the residual edge case (workspace-sync hasn't run yet, decider committed 5 minutes ago) and turn what would have been a false-positive critical into a clearly-labeled informational. Three independent payoffs: (1) integrity stops false-positiving, (2) FUSE becomes consistent for all consumers, (3) the residual race condition is handled gracefully rather than alerting.
recommendation.counterargument_acknowledged: The strongest case against B is that workspace-sync currently never writes git-owned files to FUSE; this PROP makes it a second writer (after build.js publish). If a future change to workspace-sync introduces a bug in this code path, FUSE could diverge from git in a misleading way. Mitigation: the mirror is byte-identical clone→FUSE (no transformation), so the only failure mode is 'cp didn't run' or 'wrong file' — both of which integrity's existing checks would catch on the next run. The architectural cost is small relative to the recurring false-positive cost of the status quo.

proposed_fix.summary: Three-file change: workspace-sync.md gets a small mirror loop appended to Step 3 (covers all four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files); structure-integrity.md Section 9 gets a defense-in-depth git-HEAD cross-check before flagging build_drift critical; test.js's mtime assertion is replaced with a content-equivalence check that doesn't depend on FUSE-vs-git mtime semantics.

files_to_modify.anchor: End of Step 3, immediately after the universal-pusher rebuild block (current line ~446, just before the closing 'fi' / 'git status --porcelain')
files_to_modify.find:
if node build.js html 2>&1 | tail -3; then
    git add docs/index.html 2>/dev/null
    echo "  → Staged regenerated docs/index.html (md5 $(md5sum docs/index.html | cut -c1-8)))."
  else
    echo "WARN: 'node build.js html' failed. Continuing without docs/ regen."
    echo "      The data sync will still commit; the integrity agent will catch any"
    echo "      resulting build drift on its next run (daily 09:08Z). Operator can"
    echo "      manually run 'node build.js publish' from a clone to clear it."
  fi
fi
files_to_modify.replace:
if node build.js html 2>&1 | tail -3; then
    git add docs/index.html 2>/dev/null
    echo "  → Staged regenerated docs/index.html (md5 $(md5sum docs/index.html | cut -c1-8)))."
  else
    echo "WARN: 'node build.js html' failed. Continuing without docs/ regen."
    echo "      The data sync will still commit; the integrity agent will catch any"
    echo "      resulting build drift on its next run (daily 09:08Z). Operator can"
    echo "      manually run 'node build.js publish' from a clone to clear it."
  fi
fi

# PROP-033 (FUSE-mirror): the four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files are git-owned, but
# the only existing clone→FUSE writer is build.js publish (operator-only).
# Outside of operator publishes, FUSE drifts: workspace-sync rebuilds
# docs/index.html in this clone (above) and decider commits to docs/llms.txt /
# sitemap.xml / robots.txt from its own clone — neither writes back to FUSE.
# Integrity reads FUSE for build-drift / discoverability checks and false-
# positives on every cycle that follows a non-publish push (canonical incident:
# integrity report 2026-05-13T01-16, 'critical: stale HTML' against a git tree
# that was already correct). This block mirrors the four NEVER_PUSH docs/*
# files clone → FUSE if they differ. Direction is identical to build.js publish
# for git-owned files; the smart_copy NEVER_PUSH deny-list still prevents the
# reverse direction (FUSE → git). Idempotent: cmp -s skips identical files.
for f in docs/index.html docs/llms.txt docs/sitemap.xml docs/robots.txt; do
  if [ -f "$f" ] && [ -f "${WORKSPACE}/$f" ] && ! cmp -s "$f" "${WORKSPACE}/$f"; then
    cp -f "$f" "${WORKSPACE}/$f"
    echo "  → FUSE-mirror: $f synced clone → FUSE (md5 $(md5sum "$f" | cut -c1-8))"
  elif [ -f "$f" ] && [ ! -f "${WORKSPACE}/$f" ]; then
    cp -f "$f" "${WORKSPACE}/$f"
    echo "  → FUSE-mirror: $f created on FUSE (was missing)"
  fi
done
files_to_modify.explanation: Insert the mirror loop immediately after the existing universal-pusher rebuild block. Runs every workspace-sync cycle (hourly), so max FUSE staleness on any of these four files is ~1 hour from a git change. cmp -s makes the loop a no-op when content already matches — no spurious FUSE writes. The loop covers docs/index.html (just-rebuilt by this run) and the three less-frequently-changing siblings (caught on the next cycle after a decider commit).

files_to_modify.anchor: NEVER_PUSH array comment block (current lines 59-86) — add a one-line note that these files are now also clone→FUSE mirrored
files_to_modify.replace:
# PROP-033: the four docs/* entries below remain in NEVER_PUSH (FUSE → git
# is forbidden) but ARE clone → FUSE mirrored at end of Step 3 so integrity's
# FUSE-side checks see the same content GitHub Pages serves. The deny-list
# direction is unchanged; only the forward direction is added.
NEVER_PUSH=(
  'docs/index.html'
  'docs/llms.txt'
  'docs/sitemap.xml'
  'docs/robots.txt'
files_to_modify.explanation: Documentation-only edit on the deny-list to point readers at the new clone→FUSE mirror behavior. No code semantics change here.

files_to_modify.find:
### 9. Build Reproducibility

Run `node build.js html` and diff the output against the current `docs/index.html`. If they differ, the published site doesn't match the source data. This is a critical finding.
files_to_modify.replace:
### 9. Build Reproducibility

Run `node build.js html` and diff the output against the current `docs/index.html`. If they differ, the published site MAY be stale — but FIRST cross-check against git HEAD before flagging critical (PROP-033 defense-in-depth).

**Cross-check (added 2026-05-13 per PROP-033):** before flagging build_drift critical, fetch git HEAD's docs/index.html and compare to the freshly-rebuilt content. If they match, the live site is correct and FUSE is just stale (will reconcile within ~1h on next workspace-sync mirror). Downgrade the finding to **informational** with category `fuse_stale` and note 'git HEAD docs/index.html matches fresh build; FUSE will catch up via next workspace-sync mirror'. If git HEAD ALSO differs from the fresh build, the finding stays critical (genuine drift).

```bash
# Fetch git HEAD content for cross-check (raw URL, no clone needed).
GH_HASH=$(curl -sL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/main/docs/index.html" | md5sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
FRESH_HASH=$(node build.js html >/dev/null 2>&1 && md5sum docs/index.html | cut -d' ' -f1)
FUSE_HASH=$(md5sum docs/index.html | cut -d' ' -f1)  # workspace-side
if [ "$FUSE_HASH" != "$FRESH_HASH" ]; then
  if [ "$GH_HASH" = "$FRESH_HASH" ]; then
    echo "INFORMATIONAL: docs/index.html FUSE-stale (git HEAD matches fresh build; workspace-sync mirror will reconcile within 1h)"
  else
    echo "CRITICAL: docs/index.html stale on git AND FUSE — published surface is wrong"
  fi
fi
```
files_to_modify.explanation: Section 9 hardening. The existing build-diff is preserved as the primary signal, but the severity is conditioned on git HEAD: if git is correct, downgrade to informational because workspace-sync will reconcile FUSE shortly. If git is also wrong, keep critical. Eliminates the recurring false-positive without weakening real-drift detection.

files_to_modify.anchor: Section 6 'Discoverability Infrastructure' (lines 126-139) — add the same cross-check pattern for content-based llms.txt count assertions
files_to_modify.find: - **`docs/llms.txt`**: Must exist. Check that it contains our review URL (`funwithscience.net/dome-model-review`), mentions the dome model by name, and describes the review's key findings. If the file references specific counts (e.g., "0 of 67+"), verify those counts match `wins.json` length. Flag staleness if counts are off — the social agent can fix `llms.txt` directly, but you should flag it.
files_to_modify.replace: - **`docs/llms.txt`**: Must exist. Check that it contains our review URL (`funwithscience.net/dome-model-review`), mentions the dome model by name, and describes the review's key findings. If the file references specific counts (e.g., "0 of 67+"), verify those counts match `wins.json` length. **Cross-check (PROP-033):** if FUSE llms.txt has a stale count but git HEAD's llms.txt has the correct count, downgrade to informational `fuse_stale` (workspace-sync mirror will reconcile within ~1h). Only flag a true content-staleness finding if git HEAD is also wrong.
files_to_modify.explanation: Section 6 sibling fix for llms.txt. Same pattern as Section 9: distinguish 'FUSE just hasn't synced yet' from 'live site is wrong'.

Replaces the mtime check with a deterministic md5 comparison: build a fresh HTML from current data files into a temp path, hash it, compare to FUSE's docs/index.html. If they match, no rebuild needed; if they differ, real drift. The fallback path preserves backward compatibility if build.js doesn't yet support --to. Note: this edit is OPTIONAL if the operator wants to keep the workspace-sync mirror as the sole fix and accept that test.js will be perpetually correct as soon as the mirror runs (since FUSE will match git which will match a fresh build). Recommend landing the workspace-sync mirror first; only land the test.js change if integrity / operator wants the test.js check to be self-contained.

files_to_modify.anchor: File Ownership Rules → git-owned section, the docs/* entry
files_to_modify.find: - `docs/index.html` — generated by `build.js`; regenerated on every publish.
files_to_modify.replace: - `docs/index.html` — generated by `build.js`; regenerated on every publish. **PROP-033 (2026-05-13):** workspace-sync also mirrors clone → FUSE for this file (and the three docs/* siblings below) at end of Step 3, so FUSE consumers (integrity, test.js, operator-Claude debugging) see the same content GitHub Pages serves. The reverse direction (FUSE → git) remains forbidden via NEVER_PUSH.
files_to_modify.explanation: CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules need to acknowledge workspace-sync as a second clone→FUSE writer for the four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files. The reverse direction is unchanged.

ground_truth_at_time_of_check.fuse_docs_index_html: old (matches data state from 2026-05-12T13:22Z operator publish)
ground_truth_at_time_of_check.git_HEAD_docs_index_html: fresh (matches 2026-05-12T18:05:34Z commit ccf87ec9 — the universal-pusher rebuild that landed the citation corrections)

what_integrity_actually_did.test_js_mtime_check: FAIL — FUSE docs/index.html mtime ~13:22Z < FUSE data/wins.json mtime ~17:22Z. Asserted 'HTML is not stale (modified after or with wins.json)' — fired.
what_integrity_actually_did.section_9_build_diff: FAIL — node build.js html in workspace produced fresh HTML; diff vs FUSE docs/index.html showed 24 lines (the citation corrections + date stamp). Reported critical build_drift.
what_integrity_actually_did.outcome: Critical finding emitted: 'docs/index.html stale; live site has wrong DOI in 2 places.' Live site was actually correct.

what_integrity_would_do_under_PROP_033.scenario_1_after_workspace_sync_has_fire: FUSE has been mirrored from clone (which has git HEAD content). FUSE docs/index.html now matches git HEAD. test.js mtime check passes (or under the test.js content-check edit, the md5 comparison passes deterministically). Section 9 build-diff still detects no diff. No finding emitted. CORRECT.
what_integrity_would_do_under_PROP_033.scenario_2_workspace_sync_has_not_yet_fi: FUSE docs/index.html is still stale relative to the fresh-build hash. Section 9 build-diff fires with diff > 0. NEW cross-check kicks in: curl git HEAD docs/index.html via raw URL, compute md5, compare to fresh-build md5. They MATCH (git already has the corrected HTML). Finding is downgraded from 'critical: build_drift' to 'informational: fuse_stale, git HEAD correct, workspace-sync mirror will reconcile in ≤1h'. Operator sees informational note, not a critical alert. CORRECT.
what_integrity_would_do_under_PROP_033.scenario_3_genuine_drift_e_g_data_edit_w: FUSE docs/index.html is stale, git HEAD docs/index.html is ALSO stale (no one rebuilt). Cross-check finds git HEAD also != fresh-build. Finding stays critical. Real bug surfaces. CORRECT — no degradation of true-positive detection.

retroactive_simulation.verdict: PASS. PROP-033 correctly recognizes the 2026-05-13T01:16Z case as not-stale (scenario 1 once workspace-sync has run; scenario 2 if running before workspace-sync). Real drift (scenario 3) still surfaces critical.

do_not_change.summary: Per directive task_4: this PROP is a small adjustment to align integrity's check with the existing architecture. It is NOT a redesign of file ownership or the universal-pusher flow.
preserve: File ownership model: docs/* remain 'git'-owned in build.js OWNERSHIP. The smart_copy NEVER_PUSH deny-list continues to prevent FUSE → git for these files.

build.js publish flow: operator-driven publish still does git → FUSE for all git-owned files via the existing sync-to-workspace block. PROP-033 does not duplicate or replace this; workspace-sync's mirror is an additional, smaller window of clone → FUSE coverage.
workspace-sync's universal-pusher logic (Step 3 rebuild + Step 3.5 strand rescue): unchanged. PROP-033 only appends a mirror loop after the existing rebuild stage.

Decider's commit-from-clone flow for docs/llms.txt / sitemap.xml / robots.txt: unchanged. The mirror loop catches these on the next workspace-sync cycle (≤1h later) without requiring decider to do its own FUSE write.

Integrity's existing checks (Section 6 discoverability, Section 9 build-reproducibility): the primary checks are unchanged. PROP-033 adds a defense-in-depth git-HEAD cross-check that downgrades the severity rather than suppressing the finding.
test.js as a file is preserved; only the one mtime assertion (lines 285-289) is replaced — and even that change is optional / fallback-safe.
explicitly_NOT_doing: NOT making integrity clone fresh per run (option A in pure form). Adds clone overhead without fixing the FUSE inconsistency for other consumers.
NOT making integrity web_fetch the live site (option C). Adds external network dependency; deployment lag introduces a new false-positive class.

NOT changing decider.md to add per-commit FUSE writes (alternative to the workspace-sync mirror). Would touch decider's commit logic in multiple places and add direction-coupling that's better consolidated in workspace-sync.
NOT extending the mirror to docs/index.html.baseline, docs/google*.html, or docs/fonts/*. These are out of NEVER_PUSH and have no integrity check that depends on FUSE freshness.

gain: Eliminates the recurring false-positive critical from integrity's docs/index.html check (canonical case: 2026-05-13T01:16Z).
Eliminates the same false-positive class for docs/llms.txt count checks, with sibling coverage.
Makes FUSE consistent with git for the four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files within ~1h of any change. Other FUSE consumers (operator-Claude debugging, test.js) benefit too.
Defense-in-depth git-HEAD cross-check turns the residual race-window false-positive into an informational note rather than an alert.
Preserves test.js as a useful pre-commit signal even outside the integrity pipeline (e.g., when operator runs it locally).
loss: Adds clone → FUSE writes for git-owned files in workspace-sync. Mitigated: same direction as build.js publish; smart_copy reverse-direction guard unchanged.
~10 lines of bash in workspace-sync.md, ~15 lines in structure-integrity.md, ~12 lines in test.js. Modest; the overall workspace-sync.md is already ~830 lines.
Defense-in-depth check adds one curl per integrity run (only when build_drift would have fired). Negligible network load.

If workspace-sync's mirror itself has a bug (wrong content or wrong path), FUSE could diverge from git silently. Mitigation: the mirror is byte-identical clone → FUSE with no transformation; failure modes are 'cp didn't run' or 'wrong file', both detectable on next integrity run.
edge_cases_considered: What if a FUSE write fails (disk full, permission)? cp returns non-zero; the loop continues to next file (no `set -e` regression). Worst case: one file isn't mirrored that cycle; integrity may flag it; next cycle retries. Acceptable.

What if workspace-sync runs during a partial decider commit? workspace-sync runs from its own clone after a fresh pull; it sees only fully-pushed state. Decider commits atomically; partial state doesn't reach git.

What if operator runs build.js publish concurrently with workspace-sync? build.js publish does git → FUSE for ALL git-owned files including these four; workspace-sync's mirror would write the same content (clone HEAD == git HEAD == fresh build). cmp -s returns equal; loop is a no-op. Idempotent.

What if the four files diverge in git (e.g., docs/index.html updated but docs/llms.txt not)? Each file is checked independently; only divergent files are mirrored. cmp -s makes the loop a precise no-op for matching files.

What if a file is missing from FUSE entirely (e.g., never operator-published since file was created)? The 'create on FUSE' branch handles that case. (Not expected currently — all four exist on FUSE per the audit table — but defensive.)

estimated_impact.incident_cost_today: One critical-severity false-positive per integrity run during the post-rebuild / pre-publish window. Erodes operator trust in the 'critical' label. The 2026-05-13T01:16Z incident required operator-Claude investigation at 05:18Z to confirm the finding was false — ~15 minutes of operator attention on a non-issue.
estimated_impact.incident_cost_amortized: If integrity false-positives once per ~3-day cycle (rough estimate from frequency of universal-pusher-without-publish patterns), and each false-positive costs ~15 minutes of operator attention, that's ~3 hours/year of wasted attention plus background trust erosion. The fix runs for free on every workspace-sync cycle thereafter.
estimated_impact.performance_overhead: workspace-sync mirror loop: 4 × cmp -s + occasional cp + md5sum. ~50ms per cycle when files match (the common case), ~200ms when one file differs. Negligible vs the existing ~10s sync runtime. structure-integrity defense-in-depth: 1 curl + 1 md5sum on the rare path (only when build_drift would fire). ~500ms occasional. Negligible.

why_human_needed: Three judgment calls: (1) operator preference is for option A; this PROP recommends hybrid B+A. The case is made above but operator should confirm. (2) The CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules edit needs operator sign-off because it's a small but real architectural extension (second clone→FUSE writer pattern). (3) The optional test.js change is a non-trivial assertion semantics swap; operator may prefer to land workspace-sync mirror first and revisit test.js separately.
verification_pattern: grep -q 'FUSE-mirror' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'PROP-033' monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md && grep -q 'PROP-033' CLAUDE.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
verification_pattern_explanation: Returns FIXED when (a) workspace-sync.md contains the 'FUSE-mirror' loop comment marker AND (b) structure-integrity.md contains the PROP-033 reference (defense-in-depth Section 9 / Section 6 edit) AND (c) CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules acknowledge the new mirror direction. The conjunction is intentional — partial implementation (e.g., only workspace-sync edit landed but integrity still references mtime as canonical) wouldn't fully resolve the reported bug. The optional test.js edit is not part of the verification gate; it can land on its own schedule.
expected_when_fixed: All three grep markers present on origin/main. Next integrity run after a non-publish workspace-sync cycle reports no critical build_drift; if a transient race occurs, the finding is labeled 'fuse_stale informational' instead of critical.
implementation_notes_for_operator_or_dec: The workspace-sync.md edit (mirror loop + NEVER_PUSH comment) is genuinely a one-shot patch — apply both finds-and-replaces, run workspace-sync once, observe the FUSE-mirror log lines for the four files. If any line shows a copy, that's the catch-up; subsequent runs should show no copies (cmp -s no-op).
The structure-integrity.md edits are two independent finds-and-replaces in Section 9 and Section 6. They can be applied together in one commit.

The test.js edit is OPTIONAL. Recommend landing the workspace-sync mirror first, observing one full day of integrity reports, and then deciding whether the test.js edit is still needed. If the workspace-sync mirror keeps FUSE in sync reliably, the existing mtime check will pass and the test.js edit can be deferred or dropped.

After landing: monitor next 3 integrity runs for any 'fuse_stale informational' notes. If they appear repeatedly on the same cycle, the workspace-sync schedule may need an extra mirror-only run between data changes and integrity's daily fire.
